questions.question_text: CO Mapping Write a short program to add two 8bit numbers of register B with 8 bit number of register L and store 8 bit result in memory location 9210 H.

a. Write a program in 8086 assembly to reverse an array of 10 bytes stored at a particular memory location. b. How is memory interfaced to 8086? Design a memory interfacing circuit for 8 KB RAM and 8 KB ROM. CO Mapping
Explain the system bus structure used in 8086. How are address, data, and control buses separated? Write a program using INT 21H to read and display a string.
Describe the steps to interface an input port and an output port using memory-mapped I/O and write related code. CO Mapping
Explain the internal structure and control word format of 8255 Programmable Peripheral Interface. How is it used for I/O interfacing?
Explain the features and operation of the USART 8251 and how it helps in asynchronous serial communication. CO Mapping

subparts.text: Compare CISC and RISC architectures in terms of instruction set complexity, execution speed, and hardware design.
Discuss I2C interfacing with respect to PIC microcontroller.

questions.question_text: Compare 8085 and 8086 microprocessors with respect to architecture, data bus, instruction set, and memory addressing.
Describe the use of timers in PIC microcontrollers. What are the common timer modes and their applications?
